executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 100+ organizations compromised in a single 48-hour period. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (hit by two separate gangs), Markham Stouffville Hospital, Eastern Townships School Board, National Money Mart (database leaked), and Peterborough Public Health. The Qilin ransomware group leads the attack wave with 20+ victims, followed by CLOP with 13 victims and Play with 9. Critical sectors affected include healthcare, education, financial services, and municipal government—indicating a coordinated exploitation campaign likely targeting a common vulnerability or supply chain weakness.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is experiencing a severe nationwide ransomware crisis with over 100 organizations compromised across all provinces in 48 hours. Critical infrastructure including healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital, Peterborough Public Health, Thunder Bay Counselling, Bison Family Medical), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart College, Collge Superieur De Montreal), municipal government (Municipality of North Perth), and financial services (National Money Mart with database leak, Morison Insurance Brokers) have been hit. No CCCS advisories were issued in this period despite the mass attack campaign.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose compromised by two separate ransomware groups (Coinbase Cartel and ShinyHunters). Active Green + Ross and Kids & Company also breached.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (payday loan/cheque cashing) suffered database leak via Everest ransomware. Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi. Multiple accounting firms hit: Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant.

report_2w.executive_summary: Canadian cybersecurity authorities (CCCS) have issued alerts for two actively exploited Fortinet vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-35616 and CVE-2026-21643) affecting FortiClientEMS, requiring immediate patching. Cisco released patches for four critical flaws in Identity Services and Webex that enable remote code execution and user impersonation, with one requiring immediate customer action. McGraw Hill suffered a massive data breach exposing 13.5 million accounts after hackers exploited a Salesforce misconfiguration, with threat group ShinyHunters claiming responsibility. Multiple critical vulnerabilities are under active exploitation including an nginx-ui authentication bypass (CVE-2026-33032) and a Microsoft SharePoint zero-day patched in April's Patch Tuesday.

canada_landscape.summary: CCCS has issued two critical alerts (AL26-007) for actively exploited Fortinet FortiClientEMS vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-35616 and CVE-2026-21643) requiring immediate patching by Canadian organizations. These represent official government advisories highlighting active threats to Canadian infrastructure. No specific incidents involving major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified in this reporting period.
